questions.question: According to Maslow, self-actualization can only become a primary motivating force when:

options.text: A person has received unconditional positive regard
A person has resolved all unconscious conflicts
Lower-level needs in the hierarchy have been substantially met

questions.explanation: Maslow's hierarchy of needs proposes that higher-level needs like self-actualization become motivating forces only after more basic needs (physiological, safety, belongingness, esteem) have been substantially satisfied.

questions.question: In Rogers' theory, a significant gap between the real self and the ideal self produces:
questions.explanation: Rogers proposed that when there is a large discrepancy between who a person actually is (real self) and who they want to be (ideal self), the result is incongruence, which leads to anxiety and dissatisfaction.
